title: The Ancient Dig Site
story_html:
Leo's science class went on a field trip to an ancient stone site in the hills. Everyone hoped to discover something new before next month's fair, where each student would compete for the best project, and the whole trip was meant to improve their understanding of local history.

Mr. Han, the teacher, gathered everyone by a low stone structure and reminded them of the safety rules before they began. "The path along that ridge is dangerous," he warned. "Loose rocks can fall without warning, so stay close to the marked trail, where it does not disappear into the trees."

Leo listened carefully, then picked up a small brush to master the gentle way of clearing dust from old stone. His friend Mei was generous and let him borrow her extra brush when his got stuck in a crack. Leo was thoughtful, too — when he uncovered a small carved face, he did not want to steal the moment for himself, so he called Mei over right away.

The carving looked strange and beautiful, its name lost to time. Mr. Han shared one fact about it: the same symbol appeared on an old map from long ago. Leo connected the carving to a story he had read once, and he felt proud of the small link he had found.

By afternoon, Leo's arm muscles ached from digging, and his throat felt tired and thirsty. He poured cool water from his bottle and drank deeply before sharing the rest with Mei. Looking at the result of their work, Leo noticed a small difference between this stone and the others, something that might mean it was even older than the rest.

Back on the bus, Leo tried to express how excited he felt about the discovery, hoping it would help the class achieve a great score at the fair.

science.def: The study of the natural world.

name.def: What a person or thing is called.

discover.def: To find something for the first time.

pour.def: To make a liquid flow out of a container.

improve.def: To make something better than it was before.

result.def: What happens because of something else.

thoughtful.def: Kind and caring, or deep in thought.

steal.def: To take something that is not yours.

muscle.def: A part inside the body that helps you move.

structure.def: The way the parts of something are put together.

master.def: To learn to do something very well.

disappear.def: To go away and not be seen.

connect.def: To join or link two things together.

listen.def: To pay attention in order to hear something.

achieve.def: To succeed in doing something after effort.

difference.def: The way two things are not the same.

generous.def: Willing to give more of something, like time or money, than is necessary.

express.def: To show your feelings or thoughts.

tired.def: Needing rest or sleep; feeling low on energy.

thirsty.def: Feeling like you need to drink something.

rule.def: An official instruction about what to do.

compete.def: To try to win against others.

questions.qtext: In "Loose rocks can fall without warning, so stay close to the marked trail, where it does not disappear into the trees," what does disappear mean?
options: To go away and not be seen
questions.explain: B — Mr. Han meant they should stay where the trail is easy to see, not where it goes out of sight among the trees.

questions.qtext: In "he did not want to steal the moment for himself, so he called Mei over right away," what does steal mean in this sentence?
options: To keep something good for yourself instead of sharing it
questions.explain: A — Leo did not want to keep the exciting discovery only for himself, so he shared it with his friend right away.

questions.qtext: In "His friend Mei was generous and let him borrow her extra brush," what does generous mean?
options: Careful and slow to trust others
Willing to give or share more than necessary
questions.explain: B — Mei shared her brush with Leo without being asked twice, which shows she is generous.

questions.qtext: Why did Mr. Han warn the students about the ridge?
options: Because it was too far from the bus
Because loose rocks there could fall and it was dangerous
Because the ancient carvings were hidden there
Because it was against the fair's rules to go there
questions.explain: B — he said the path was dangerous because loose rocks could fall without warning.

questions.qtext: What did Leo notice about the stone he uncovered?
options: It was exactly the same as every other stone there
It had a small difference that might mean it was older than the rest
It was too heavy for him to lift
It had been broken by loose falling rocks
questions.explain: B — Leo noticed a small difference between his stone and the others, which might mean it was even older.
